data.basisOfEstimate: This is a formal RFP (RFP No. 26-22) from San Bernardino City Unified School District seeking a hosted, integrated data warehouse and business intelligence/analytics platform solution. NO government estimated value is provided in the solicitation. Organization labor rates are provided (Software Engineer: $171.40/hr, Manufacturing Engineer: $178.89/hr, QA: $16.86/hr) but are IRRELEVANT because this solicitation seeks an existing commercial SaaS product, not custom software development services. The pricing structure is product-based: per-student annual licensing (~44,000 students), hosting fees, one-time implementation fees, and training hours. HORUSTECH is a custom software development firm with AWS expertise but does NOT have an existing K-12 data warehouse/BI platform product. The District is currently using PowerSchool Analytics/Unified Insights and is seeking a replacement or upgrade — competitors will be established EdTech BI vendors (e.g., Tableau/Salesforce Education, PowerSchool, Schoolzilla/Renaissance, Clever Analytics, Otus, Panorama Education, etc.) with mature products and per-student pricing models. The organization's past performance includes an educational data platform for DegreeData ($800K) focused on document processing/credential verification, which is tangentially related but fundamentally different from a district-wide BI/analytics platform. Total price is set to $0 because the organization cannot competitively price an existing product it does not have. Price confidence is set to 18 (very low) reflecting the fundamental industry/product mismatch.
assumptions: This solicitation requires an existing commercial data warehouse and BI platform product, not custom software development services.
The competitive field will consist of established K-12 EdTech BI vendors with mature products and per-student pricing models.

Typical K-12 data warehouse/BI platform pricing for a district of 44,000 students ranges from $2-$8 per student per year for licensing, plus implementation costs — but this is general market knowledge, not a firm estimate.

A 5-year total cost of ownership for a district this size from an established vendor would likely range from $500K to $2M depending on the vendor and scope of services — this is speculative and based on general EdTech market awareness.
HORUSTECH's organization labor rates are not applicable to this solicitation's pricing model.
No government estimate was provided in the solicitation.

The organization has no existing K-12 data warehouse/BI product, no K-12 school district past performance, and no pre-built integrations with the required K-12 data sources (Aeries, CAASPP, ELPAC, etc.).
Building a competitive product from scratch to respond to this RFP is not feasible within the timeline or at a competitive price point.
pricingRisks: CRITICAL INDUSTRY MISMATCH: This RFP seeks a mature, hosted data warehouse and BI/analytics platform product (similar to PowerSchool Analytics, Tableau, Qlik, or Schoolzilla). HORUSTECH is a custom software development firm, not a product vendor with an existing K-12 data warehouse/BI SaaS platform.

The solicitation requires a turnkey product with pre-built integrations to 20+ K-12 data sources (Aeries, CAASPP, ELPAC, NWEA MAP, Illuminate, SEIS, etc.). Building these integrations from scratch would be cost-prohibitive and non-competitive.
The District is replacing PowerSchool Analytics/Unified Insights — competitors are established EdTech BI vendors with existing products, not custom development shops.
Cost is the most heavily weighted criterion at 40 points. Established SaaS vendors with per-student pricing models will have significant cost advantages over a custom-build approach.
FERPA compliance, SBCUSD-specific data quality rules, and K-12 education domain expertise are required — the organization has no demonstrated K-12 education data compliance track record.
The 5-year contract term (1 base + 4 option years) with annual per-student licensing model favors established product vendors with economies of scale.

Firm experience with School Districts similar in size and scope to SBCUSD (44,000 students, 74 schools) is a 15-point evaluation criterion — no evidence of K-12 school district experience exists in the knowledge base.
The pricing structure (per-student annual license, hosting fees, implementation fees) is designed for SaaS product vendors, not custom development firms.
recommendedActions: STRONG NO-BID RECOMMENDATION: This solicitation seeks an existing commercial data warehouse and BI platform product for K-12 education. HORUSTECH does not have such a product. Bidding would require building a platform from scratch, which is not competitive against established EdTech vendors.
If pursuing despite mismatch, consider partnering with or subcontracting to an established K-12 data warehouse/BI vendor and providing implementation/customization services.
Monitor future SBCUSD solicitations for custom software development, data integration, or cloud migration services where HORUSTECH's AWS expertise would be directly applicable.
Explore the K-12 EdTech market for potential partnership opportunities with BI platform vendors who may need AWS cloud infrastructure or custom integration development support.
If the organization wants to enter the K-12 BI market long-term, invest in building a product first before responding to RFPs of this nature.

evaluationFactors: Cost of Services (including annual renewal, hosting fees, data integration fees, software maintenance fees, support and training, all other fees) - 40 points
Responses to Scope of Work and Supplemental Questionnaire - 25 points
Firm's expertise, background, personnel qualifications, and experience with School Districts similar in size and scope to SBCUSD - 15 points
Proposal Format, Organization, and Clarity - 5 points
data.overview: San Bernardino City Unified School District (SBCUSD) RFP No. 26-22 seeks a 100% hosted integrated data warehouse and business intelligence (BI)/analytics platform solution. The platform must store and integrate data from various student and staff data sources, support creation and dissemination of reports and dashboards, and increase capacity for timely, accurate, data-driven decision making across the District's approximately 44,000 students and 74 schools. The initial contract term is one year with up to four one-year extensions (five years total). Success means a fully integrated, scalable data warehouse with BI capabilities supporting program evaluation, KPI monitoring, early warning systems, MTSS/RTI, and college/career readiness tracking.

requirements.requirement: Provide a 100% hosted integrated data warehouse and business intelligence (BI)/analytics platform
Shall be compliant with all federal, state, and SBCUSD policies, laws, and regulations
Shall include sufficient licenses for implementation District-wide (approximately 44,000 students)
Shall include all future software upgrades and enhancements
Ability to track student data including assessment, academic performance, demographics, attendance, program participation, behavior, interventions and student plans
Ability to track staff data including attendance, assignment and demographics
Easily import state mandated assessment results (e.g., CAASPP, ELPAC)
Easily import other student assessments unique to secondary schools (e.g., AP, IB)
Easily import other student assessments unique to SBCUSD (e.g., Standards-Aligned Assessments, formative assessments)
Ability to track and report National Student Clearinghouse (NSC) data to monitor postsecondary enrollment and completion outcomes
Describe if the system uses any third-party software and level of support provided
Support for both Windows and Mac
Compatible with District server hardware (HP)
Compatibility with mobile device platforms
Ability for technical staff to connect and query the data warehouse using industry-standard tools and query languages
Multi-level security systems for transmission, storage and reporting of data and end-user access
Catastrophic event recovery contingency plan with turnaround less than 1 week
User authentication via Active Directory (Desired)
Ability to monitor and download (preferably into CSV) application usage data from end-users such as user traffic to BI views
Role-based security options giving SBCUSD maximum flexibility in assigning roles to users and administrators
Possibility of data scrambling or visualizations with made-up data for training purposes (Desired)
Data quality checks to ensure accuracy of data in the system
Report out data quality issues segregated by specific business owner groups with communication methods (e.g., push email notifications, daily BI reports)
Ability to query data and extract the data that is queried
Provide information on the matching process employed for student data to ensure accuracy of student-level data combined from various sources
Implement and maintain data quality checks unique to SBCUSD between two or more data elements (Desired)
Reporting at student, classroom, teacher, site and District levels with aggregated metrics and drill-down to student or row-level detail by security access level
Capacity for report and data exports to other formats (e.g., Excel, Access)
Reports in PDF format for printing with minimal customization from end-users
Ability to create both static and dynamic reporting
Customizable reporting tool that supports complex queries (e.g., Boolean Logic) (Desired)
Capacity to create a variety of visualization types including bar charts, line charts, Venn diagrams, scatter plots, and crosstabs
Allow District staff to generate and disseminate customized reports and data dashboards for authenticated users and for the public
Ability to show time-based or longitudinal measures
Ability to perform GIS mapping (Desired)
Ability to incorporate interactive features into dashboards (filters, highlights, dropdowns, single or multi-select)
Ability for users to download data used to create an aggregate measure into CSV format
Ability for users to generate custom groupings of students for targeted analysis, unique to end-user or shared amongst end-users
Ability to embed reports or dashboards into District website
Ability to integrate or embed other BI dashboards currently designed by the District (Desired)
Ability to include instructions or help documents to guide user interaction with reports and dashboards (Desired)
Allows for branding customization by the District (logos, fonts, color schemes) (Desired)
Pre-built reports or metrics readily usable by the District following data validation (Desired)
Analytics capabilities including advanced analytics such as predictive analytics and inferential statistics (Desired)
Ability to incorporate language translation of reports or dashboards
Ability to perform natural language querying allowing users to generate visualizations and reports by typing plain-language questions
Provide training on designing, building, and troubleshooting metrics compiled from the data warehouse
Provide training to identified users on how to build visualizations, reports, and dashboards and optimize performance
Provide training to identified users on how to find and correct data quality issues reported by the system
Vendor-supplied technical support and staff/vendor-staffed help-desk
Quality online help documents and job-aids
BI system capable of embedding training materials for end users (Desired)
Vendor-provided workshops for selected staff such as training-of-trainers (Desired)
Training to end users on how to use visualizations, reports and dashboards; training materials subject to review and approval by SBCUSD
Ability to export/store data in a variety of regional databases
Capacity for the system to be hosted by District if deemed desirable by SBCUSD
Capacity for District to access the database with backend data analysis tools
Provide an implementation plan and project schedule showing critical milestones with tasks, beginning and end dates, and responsible parties
During implementation, provide weekly progress reports summarizing actions, issues, resolutions, deadlines, or tasks at risk (Desired)
Clearly define SBCUSD and Offeror responsibilities for implementation and on-going maintenance
Testing environment available to client for testing (Desired)
Email protocols or other notification features to communicate issues about system performance, health, data issues, quality issues (Desired)
Reports or other abilities in monitoring system performance including resources, processes, and jobs
Plan to address fault tolerance, high availability, replication strategy, on-site replication options, stress testing, load balancing, and estimated recovery time for multiple scenarios
Capacity for interface/integration with District Student Information System AERIES

Ability to integrate with data sources listed in Table 1 (Aeries, CAASPP, ELPAC, NWEA MAP, Illuminate, SEIS, itsLearning, Renaissance STAR, DIBELS, College Board Suite, IXL, NSC, CCGI, CSAC, Google Suite, Apex Learning, Edmentum, IB, CA Physical Fitness Test, CA Dept of Education Research Files, survey data, District Adopted Curriculum Digital Components)
Integration with SBCUSD's District-Adopted curricula (Desired)
Ability to integrate data sources unique to SBCUSD beyond those listed, requiring minimal conforming from existing data layouts
Nightly student roster refreshing
On-demand and real-time demographic data refreshing
Ability to provide client with scripts for data integration with AERIES
Update data integration regularly to match state and national data layouts without intervention from SBCUSD
Intuitive, simple and user-friendly tools for end users
Provide updates that are easy to access and install
Import 5 years of historical data
Provide documentation on all stored procedures and functions including purpose, parameters, and usage samples
Provide on-going technical support for development of new metrics, integration of new data sources, and performance of the system
Data lake capabilities enabling SBCUSD to securely upload, store, and manage ad hoc datasets from various sources supporting structured and unstructured data formats
Solution should support interoperability, reusable datasets, and efficient data exploration while minimizing duplicative or parallel data pipelines

Enable the District to design and manage intervention programs, provide dashboards evaluating intervention effectiveness, and support creation of individualized student plans for continuous progress monitoring
Comply with FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act) and California Education Code Section 49073.1 (AB 1584)
Comply with Workers' Compensation requirements per California Labor Code Section 3700
Certification regarding Debarment, Suspension, Ineligibility and Voluntary Exclusion
Iran Contracting Act Certification per Public Contract Code Section 2204
Federal Funding Contract Compliance (EDGAR) requirements
Provide resumes or profiles of key personnel assigned including Project Leader, Implementation Team, and Customer Service Key Contact
Vendor must have been in business under the same license and company name for at least five consecutive years and have completed at least five similar projects
Maintain Commercial General Liability insurance with combined single limits not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate
Maintain Commercial Automobile Liability insurance with combined single limits not less than $1,000,000 each occurrence
Maintain Professional Errors and Omissions insurance not less than $1,000,000 per claim/$2,000,000 aggregate
Maintain Cyber Security and Privacy Liability insurance with combined single limits not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate
Maintain Workers' Compensation insurance as required under California Labor Code with Employers Liability not less than $1,000,000 per accident/injury/disease
Pricing shall be held firm for the duration of the contract except for inflationary adjustment approved by the District annually
No proposal may be withdrawn for a period of sixty (60) days after stated due date

format: Electronic submission via OpenGov e-Procurement portal at https://procurement.opengov.com/portal/sbcusd
Sealed pricing - pricing must NOT be included in the main proposal upload (10 point penalty if pricing is included in the proposal section)
Fee proposal must be uploaded separately from the main proposal
All pricing tables should be completed online through the OpenGov e-Procurement portal Pricing Table Section 8
Limit all pricing figures to two (2) decimal points
Proposals must be submitted electronically no later than Friday, March 20, 2026 at 11:00 AM PST
No proposals accepted after closing date and time
attachmentsAndForms: F - Certification Regarding Debarment, Suspension, Ineligibility & Voluntary Exclusion
H - Education Department of General Administration Regulation (EDGAR) Federal Funding Compliance Form
J - Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA)
K - Conflict of Interest and Disclosures
L - Signed District Agreement Form (upon award)

requiredDocuments.name: Cover Letter/Letter of Interest
requiredDocuments.description: Letter stating eligibility, brief description of respondent, and statement of interest

requiredDocuments.name: Proposal (without Fee Proposal or Pricing)
requiredDocuments.description: Complete proposal including Sections A through I: Cover Letter, Description of Company, Personnel and Staffing Resources, Capacity and Methodology, Experience and References, Qualification Statement, Customer Service Commitment, and Exceptions. Must NOT include pricing.

requiredDocuments.name: Fee Proposal / Price Breakdown
requiredDocuments.description: Separate fee proposal with comprehensive line item cost breakdown uploaded separately from the main proposal. Includes Year 1-5 pricing, annual license fees, hosting fees, implementation fees, training costs, and rate sheet.

requiredDocuments.name: Qualification Statement
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Qualification Statement form (Attachment A) including background, staff qualifications, vendor facility, customer service approach, disaster recovery, security compliance, implementation plan, warranty, litigation history, and references

requiredDocuments.name: Experience and References
requiredDocuments.description: Description of past performances of similar service with list of school districts or county offices of education served in last 5 years, including number of students, dates, client name, and contact information. At least three client references required.

requiredDocuments.name: Vendor Response Table (Attachment M)
requiredDocuments.description: Table indicating whether firm can meet or exceed each Required (R) or Desired (D) feature listed in the Scope of Work

requiredDocuments.name: Non-Collusion Affidavit
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Non-Collusion Affidavit per Public Contract Code Section 7106 (Attachment B)

requiredDocuments.name: W-9 Tax Form
requiredDocuments.description: Completed IRS W-9 Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification (Attachment D)

requiredDocuments.name: Equal Opportunity Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Equal Opportunity Certification form (Attachment E)

requiredDocuments.name: Certification Regarding Debarment, Suspension, Ineligibility & Voluntary Exclusion
Workers' Compensation Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Workers' Compensation Certification per California Labor Code Section 3700 (Attachment G)

requiredDocuments.name: Federal Funding Contract Compliance Form (EDGAR)
requiredDocuments.description: Completed EDGAR Federal Funding Compliance Form (Attachment H)

requiredDocuments.name: Iran Contracting Act Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Iran Contracting Act Certification per Public Contract Code Section 2204 (Attachment I)

requiredDocuments.name: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) Form
requiredDocuments.description: Completed FERPA compliance form per California Education Code Section 49073.1 (Attachment J)

requiredDocuments.name: Conflict of Interest and Disclosures
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Conflict of Interest and Disclosures form (Attachment K)

requiredDocuments.name: Exceptions to Sample Agreement and Terms & Conditions
requiredDocuments.description: Any exceptions to District Terms & Conditions, software license agreements, contract modifications, deletions or additions. Optional; no response assumes acceptance of all terms.

requiredVolumes: Section A - Cover Letter/Letter of Interest
Section C - Personnel and Staffing Resources
Section G - Fiscal Stability (if requested by District)

deliverables: 100% hosted integrated data warehouse and BI/analytics platform
Implementation plan and project schedule with critical milestones, tasks, dates, and responsible parties
Training on designing, building, and troubleshooting metrics from the data warehouse
Training to users on building visualizations, reports, and dashboards
Training to users on finding and correcting data quality issues
Training to end users on using visualizations, reports and dashboards (materials subject to SBCUSD review and approval)
Documentation on all stored procedures and functions including purpose, parameters, and usage samples
Plan addressing fault tolerance, high availability, replication strategy, stress testing, load balancing, and recovery time estimates
Scripts for data integration with AERIES
Import of 5 years of historical data
Clearly defined SBCUSD and Offeror responsibilities for implementation and on-going maintenance
Fee Proposal Form with comprehensive line item cost breakdown

redFlags.whyItMatters: The Scope of Work explicitly describes the current PowerSchool environment with 'District specific customizations, completed historical data migration, and automated nightly data refreshes.' PowerSchool (the vendor) has deep institutional knowledge of SBCUSD's data environment and existing integrations. Any competing vendor must overcome switching costs, data migration risk, and retraining of District staff already familiar with the current platform.
redFlags.mitigation: Position the proposal around the District's stated desire to 'enhance and expand' toward 'a more flexible and scalable architecture that incorporates data lake functionality' and 'third-party business intelligence platforms' — capabilities the District implies the current solution may lack. Emphasize modern architecture advantages.
redFlags.flag: Current platform is PowerSchool Analytics and Insights — strong incumbent product advantage

redFlags.whyItMatters: The evaluation criteria allocates 15 points to 'Firm's expertise, background, personnel qualifications, and experience with School Districts similar in size and scope to SBCUSD' (44,000 students, 74 schools). The RFP requires references from 'other school districts or county offices of education' of 'approximate size of the District' served in the last 5 years. HORUSTECH's past performance does not appear to include K-12 school district data warehouse implementations.
redFlags.mitigation: If no direct K-12 district references exist, this is a significant competitive disadvantage. Consider partnering with a firm that has established K-12 data warehouse references. Without such references, a no-bid decision may be warranted.
redFlags.flag: Limited K-12 school district references of similar size and scope

redFlags.whyItMatters: The OpenGov portal submission instructions explicitly state: 'There will be a 10 point penalty if pricing is included in this section' (Vendor Submission Item 3). This is a sealed pricing process where pricing must be uploaded separately in Item 4.
redFlags.mitigation: Carefully review the entire proposal document before submission to ensure no pricing information appears in the technical proposal upload. Have a second reviewer verify separation of pricing and technical content.
redFlags.flag: 10-point penalty for including pricing in the wrong section

risks.whyItMatters: The solicitation explicitly states: 'The District's current environment utilizes PowerSchool Analytics and Insights (formerly Unified Insights) as its data warehouse and analytics platform. The implementation includes District specific customizations, completed historical data migration, and automated nightly data refreshes.' Any new vendor must replicate or migrate these customizations, historical data, and integrations, giving the current platform provider a significant advantage in cost, risk, and transition effort.
risks.mitigation: Develop a detailed migration and transition plan addressing historical data import (5 years required per F.12), custom configurations, and nightly refresh continuity. Highlight risks of staying with incumbent vs. benefits of a modern architecture.
risks.flag: Existing incumbent platform (PowerSchool Analytics and Insights) with established customizations and data migration

risks.whyItMatters: RFP posted February 27, 2026 with proposals due March 20, 2026 — approximately 21 calendar days. Given the extensive requirements (60+ Required/Desired features across 6 categories, 13 required attachments, detailed pricing tables for 5 years, qualification statement, and references), this is a tight timeline to prepare a comprehensive and competitive response.
risks.mitigation: Begin proposal development immediately. Assign dedicated team members to compliance/attachments vs. technical response. Use the clarification deadline (March 13) to resolve ambiguities early.

risks.whyItMatters: The RFP requires integration with 20+ specific K-12 data sources (Aeries SIS, CAASPP, ELPAC, SEIS, Illuminate, NWEA MAP, College Board Suite, Renaissance STAR, itsLearning, IXL, National Student Clearinghouse, etc.). Several are marked as 'Critical Data Sources.' HORUSTECH's past performance includes educational data platform work (DegreeData) but not K-12 SIS/assessment data warehouse integration at this scale. The evaluation criteria allocates 15 points to 'experience with School Districts similar in size and scope to SBCUSD.'
risks.mitigation: Identify and partner with or subcontract to a firm with proven K-12 data warehouse integration experience, particularly with Aeries SIS. Emphasize the DegreeData project's educational data processing capabilities and AWS architecture expertise as transferable skills.
risks.flag: Extensive K-12 data source integration requirements with limited directly relevant past performance

risks.whyItMatters: The solicitation states 'price will be one of the most heavily weighted criteria' and assigns 40 out of 100 points to Cost of Services. Established K-12 EdTech vendors (e.g., PowerSchool, Illuminate, Schoolzilla/NWEA) likely have economies of scale and existing per-student pricing models. A new entrant building a custom solution may struggle to compete on price against SaaS platforms already serving similar districts.
risks.mitigation: Develop competitive per-student pricing. Consider phased implementation to reduce Year 1 costs. Clearly itemize all fees as required to avoid rejection for incomplete pricing breakdown.
risks.flag: Cost is the most heavily weighted evaluation criterion at 40%

risks.whyItMatters: Section 7.4 requires Cyber Security and Privacy Liability insurance with limits of not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate, covering PII, PHI, network security liability, notification costs, crisis management, and regulatory fines. This is a specialized policy that may not be currently held and can be costly to obtain.
risks.mitigation: Contact insurance broker immediately to confirm availability and cost of cyber liability coverage meeting these specific requirements. Factor premium costs into pricing.
risks.flag: Cyber Security and Privacy Liability insurance requirement ($1M/$2M)

risks.whyItMatters: Section 6.12 states: 'The Respondent, by submitting a RFP, indicates that they have the ability, fitness and expertise to fulfill the terms and conditions of the RFP, and has been successful in services associated with this RFP for a period of at least (5) years.' Additionally, the Supplemental Questionnaire (Section 6.3) requires 'proof that the bidder has been in business under the same license and company name for at least five consecutive years and shall have successfully completed at least five (5) similar projects.' Failure to meet this may result in bid rejection.
risks.mitigation: Verify HORUSTECH has been operating under its current name and license for at least 5 consecutive years. Compile a list of 5+ similar projects. If the company is newer, consider whether a teaming arrangement with an established firm could satisfy this requirement.

risks.whyItMatters: Section 5.6 states the District may shortlist bidders for virtual presentations in April 2026, and 'Firms invited for oral presentations must be represented by the individual who will be the prime contact person to the District, and other key project team members. A Proposer's inability to respond to any request for clarification and/or oral presentation may be cause for disqualification.'
risks.mitigation: Identify and commit the prime contact and key project team members now. Ensure their availability throughout April 2026 for potential presentation scheduling.
risks.flag: Potential oral presentation requirement with key personnel availability

risks.whyItMatters: The RFP requires 13 separate attachments (Qualification Statement, Non-Collusion Affidavit, Vendor Application, W-9, Equal Opportunity Certification, Debarment Certification, Workers' Comp Certification, EDGAR form, Iran Contracting Act, FERPA, Conflict of Interest, Signed Agreement, Vendor Response Table). Missing any required document may result in rejection per Section 5.1.
risks.mitigation: Create a compliance checklist and assign a team member to complete and verify all 13 attachments well before the March 20 deadline. Use the OpenGov portal early to identify any upload issues.

risks.whyItMatters: The RFP requires FERPA compliance and AB 1584 (California Education Code Section 49073.1) certification. The solution will handle sensitive student PII including demographics, attendance, behavior, assessments, and special education data (SEIS). Non-compliance could void the contract per the FERPA attachment terms.
risks.mitigation: Ensure the proposed solution architecture includes FERPA-compliant data handling, storage, and access controls. Document data governance procedures and student data deletion/return processes in the proposal.
risks.flag: FERPA compliance and student data privacy obligations

incumbentInfo.notes: The solicitation identifies PowerSchool Analytics and Insights (formerly Unified Insights) as the current product/platform in use, but does not name the contracting entity or incumbent contractor. PowerSchool is the product vendor, not necessarily the contract holder. The solicitation does not use terms like 'recompete', 'follow-on', or 'predecessor contract.' However, the detailed description of the current environment strongly suggests an existing contract relationship with PowerSchool or a reseller, giving that vendor a significant competitive advantage.

data.confidenceExplanation: Confidence is 55 due to: no past performance data available (-20), fundamental industry mismatch between company capabilities and solicitation requirements (-15), and expired deadline confirmed (-10). The scoring is high-confidence in its NO_GO conclusion — the gaps are clear and well-documented across all data sources. The low confidence number reflects missing data that would be needed to score higher, not uncertainty about the decision.
requiredActions: Do NOT pursue this opportunity — deadline has passed and fundamental product mismatch exists
Monitor future SBCUSD solicitations for custom software development, data integration, or cloud migration services where AWS expertise is directly applicable
If long-term K-12 EdTech market entry is desired, invest in building a product first before responding to RFPs of this nature
Explore potential partnership opportunities with established K-12 BI platform vendors who may need AWS cloud infrastructure or custom integration development support for future opportunities

criteria.name: TECHNICAL_FIT
criteria.rationale: HORUSTECH is a custom software development firm with AWS expertise but does NOT have an existing K-12 data warehouse or BI/analytics platform product. The RFP requires a 100% hosted, turnkey integrated data warehouse and BI platform with pre-built integrations to 20+ K-12-specific data sources (Aeries SIS, CAASPP, ELPAC, SEIS, NWEA MAP, Illuminate, etc.). The KB shows capabilities in serverless AWS architecture, Node.js/TypeScript development, and document processing — none of which constitute a ready-made data warehouse/BI product. The company has no pre-built K-12 data connectors, no BI reporting/dashboard product, no data lake product, no natural language querying capability, and no FERPA-compliant student data platform. This is a fundamental product mismatch: the RFP seeks an existing commercial SaaS product, not custom development services.
gaps: No existing data warehouse or BI/analytics platform product
No pre-built integrations with any of the 20+ required K-12 data sources (Aeries, CAASPP, ELPAC, SEIS, etc.)
No BI reporting, dashboard, or visualization product capabilities
No data lake product or capability
No natural language querying feature
No FERPA compliance track record or student data privacy experience
No K-12 education domain expertise documented in KB
No per-student SaaS licensing model or product pricing structure
No pre-built reports, early warning systems, MTSS/RTI, or intervention tracking features

criteria.name: PAST_PERFORMANCE_RELEVANCE
criteria.rationale: No past performance data available. The only tangentially related project is DegreeData ($800K), which involved academic document processing and credential verification — fundamentally different from a K-12 district-wide data warehouse and BI platform. HORUSTECH has zero K-12 school district clients, zero data warehouse/BI platform implementations, and zero references from school districts of any size. The RFP explicitly requires references from school districts similar in size to SBCUSD (44,000 students) served in the last 5 years, and mandates at least 5 similar completed projects. This requirement alone would likely result in bid rejection.
gaps: Zero K-12 school district past performance projects
Zero data warehouse or BI platform implementations of any kind
Cannot provide required 3 school district references
Cannot demonstrate 5 similar completed projects as required by Section 6.3
No experience with Aeries SIS integration or any K-12 data source integration
DegreeData project is document processing/credential verification, not data warehouse/BI

criteria.name: PRICING_POSITION
criteria.rationale: Pricing analysis confirms critical industry mismatch with price confidence of 18/100. HORUSTECH has no existing product to price — the RFP requires per-student annual licensing for 44,000 students, hosting fees, and implementation fees structured for a SaaS product vendor. Competitors are established EdTech BI vendors (PowerSchool, Schoolzilla, Panorama Education, etc.) with mature per-student pricing models and economies of scale. Cost is the most heavily weighted criterion at 40 points. Building a custom solution from scratch cannot compete on price against existing commercial platforms. The pricing module explicitly recommends a strong no-bid.
gaps: No existing product to price competitively
No per-student SaaS pricing model
Cannot compete against established EdTech vendors with economies of scale on the 40-point cost criterion
Custom development approach would be cost-prohibitive vs. existing commercial platforms
Organization labor rates ($171/hr) are irrelevant to this product-based pricing structure

criteria.name: STRATEGIC_ALIGNMENT
criteria.rationale: No documented strategic plan to enter the K-12 EdTech data warehouse/BI market exists in the KB or organization context. HORUSTECH's documented capabilities are in custom software development, AWS serverless architecture, and document processing — a completely different market segment from K-12 education data warehouse SaaS products. Pursuing this RFP would require building an entirely new product from scratch, which is not feasible within the response timeline or at a competitive price. There is no evidence of strategic intent to develop a K-12 BI product. The pricing analysis recommends monitoring future SBCUSD solicitations for custom development work where AWS expertise would be applicable instead.
gaps: No documented strategy to enter K-12 EdTech BI market
Fundamental industry mismatch — custom dev firm vs. SaaS product vendor requirement
No existing product foundation to build upon
Would require years of product development investment before being competitive in this space

criteria.name: INCUMBENT_RISK
criteria.rationale: PowerSchool Analytics and Insights is the current platform with established District-specific customizations, completed historical data migration, and automated nightly data refreshes. PowerSchool has deep institutional knowledge of SBCUSD's data environment and existing integrations with Aeries SIS and other K-12 data sources. The District staff is already trained on the current platform. Any competing vendor faces significant switching costs, data migration risk, and retraining burden. PowerSchool or its reseller has a dominant incumbent advantage that would be extremely difficult for even an established competitor to overcome, let alone a firm with no K-12 BI product.
gaps: PowerSchool has established customizations and data migration already completed
District staff already trained on incumbent platform
Switching costs and migration risk strongly favor incumbent
HORUSTECH has no product to even compete against the incumbent

data.summaryJustification: This is a clear NO_GO. HORUSTECH is a custom software development firm being evaluated against an RFP that requires an existing commercial K-12 data warehouse and BI/analytics SaaS platform product. There is a fundamental industry and product mismatch across every evaluation dimension. The company has no K-12 data warehouse/BI product, no K-12 school district past performance, no ability to price competitively against established EdTech vendors, no strategic alignment with this market, and faces a dominant PowerSchool incumbent. The submission deadline of March 20, 2026 has already passed (today is April 19, 2026), making submission impossible regardless. The pricing module's own analysis recommends a 'STRONG NO-BID' and assigns a price confidence of only 18/100. The RFP's mandatory requirement of 5 years in business with 5 similar completed projects would likely result in bid rejection even if all other gaps could be addressed.
blockers: CRITICAL: Submission deadline (March 20, 2026) has already passed — today is April 19, 2026
CRITICAL: No existing K-12 data warehouse or BI/analytics platform product to offer
CRITICAL: Cannot meet mandatory requirement of 5 similar completed projects (Section 6.3)
CRITICAL: Cannot provide required 3 school district references of similar size and scope
CRITICAL: No pre-built integrations with any of the 20+ required K-12 data sources
Cannot compete on price (40% of evaluation) against established SaaS EdTech vendors
data.decisionRationale: NO_GO decision driven by multiple critical and irrecoverable factors: (1) The submission deadline of March 20, 2026 has already passed — it is now April 19, 2026, making submission impossible. (2) Fundamental product mismatch — HORUSTECH is a custom software development firm with no existing K-12 data warehouse or BI platform product; the RFP requires a turnkey commercial SaaS solution. (3) No past performance — zero K-12 school district clients, zero data warehouse/BI implementations, and inability to meet the mandatory requirement of 5 similar completed projects and 3 school district references. (4) No competitive pricing position — cannot price a non-existent product against established EdTech vendors on the most heavily weighted criterion (40 points). (5) Strong PowerSchool incumbent with established customizations. All five scoring dimensions received the minimum score of 1, yielding a composite of 1.0. There are no realistic mitigations that could make this opportunity viable.

confidenceDrivers.factor: No past performance data available for K-12 data warehouse/BI
Fundamental industry mismatch: custom dev firm vs. SaaS product vendor requirement
Submission deadline has already passed (March 20 vs. today April 19)
Pricing analysis explicitly recommends strong no-bid with 18/100 confidence
Company has general AWS and serverless architecture expertise

data.summary: San Bernardino City Unified School District (SBCUSD), the ninth-largest school district in California serving approximately 44,000 students, is seeking a fully hosted integrated data warehouse and business intelligence (BI)/analytics platform. The solution must integrate data from multiple student information sources (including Aeries SIS, CAASPP, ELPAC, and numerous other assessment and curriculum platforms), provide robust reporting and dashboard capabilities, and support data lake functionality for structured and unstructured data. The platform will support district-wide decision-making initiatives including program evaluation, early warning systems, MTSS/RTI, college and career readiness monitoring, and student intervention tracking.
data.periodOfPerformance: Base year + 4 optional one-year extensions (up to 5 years total)
data.agency: San Bernardino City Unified School District
data.title: Data Management and Business Intelligence Platform Solution
